multispectral_drone_svh — results report

Gemmell et al., Applying the spectral variability hypothesis to arid shrublands

2026-05-25

Overview

This report is the canonical results document for the analysis pipeline driven by _targets.R. It loads every figure and table from cached targets via tar_read(), so it will rebuild only when an upstream target changes — re-running it requires only a working _targets/ cache (i.e. after targets::tar_make() has succeeded at least once).

For background, see the top-level README. For the workflow as code (84 targets, the live DAG), targets::tar_visnetwork().

Workflow

Figure 1. Analysis workflow from preprocessing through to manuscript figures. The preprocessing block (above the dashed line in the README version of this diagram) is upstream of the targets pipeline and produces the Zenodo-archived rasters; see preprocessing/ for those scripts.

Figure 5 — spectral vs taxonomic diversity

Per-subplot scatter plots of taxonomic diversity (species richness S, exp(H’), 1/D, Pielou J’) against each of the three continuous spectral metrics (coefficient of variation CV, spectral variance SV, log convex-hull volume log CHV), with significant-relationship overlay lines drawn from the cached glmmTMB / lm fits.

Figure 5. Spectral vs taxonomic diversity across the four arid-NSW sites.

Figure 6 — CV across band combinations

Beta coefficients (with 95% CI) for CV computed over three reduced band subsets vs the four taxonomic diversity metrics.

Figure 6. CV beta coefficients across band combinations.

Model summary tables

Continuous spectral metrics × taxonomic diversity

Mixed models (glmmTMB, site as random intercept) for each of the 12 taxonomic × spectral pairs. The three pielou_evenness rows are refit as fixed-effect linear models because the mixed specification is singular (site σ ≈ 0); they’re tagged model_kind = "fixed".

taxonomic spectral kind β 95% CI lower 95% CI upper p R² marginal R² conditional

inv_simpson log.CHV mixed -0.372 -1.029 0.284 2.66e-01 0.023 0.458
